prereviewOf.description: Part of the page title for the review-a-preprint flow.

havingProblems.description: Heading for the page that appears when there is a problem publishing a PREreview.

ownPreprint.message: Sorry, you can’t review your own preprint
ownPreprint.description: Heading for the page that appears when a user attempts to review their own preprint.

unableToPublish.message: We were unable to publish your PREreview. We saved your work.
unableToPublish.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “Sorry, we’re having problems”.

tryAgainLater.message: Please try again later by coming back to this page.
tryAgainLater.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “Sorry, we’re having problems”.

ifNotAuthor.message: If you’re not an author, please <contact>get in touch</contact>.
ifNotAuthor.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “Sorry, you can’t review your own preprint”.

getInTouch.message: If this problem persists, please <contact>get in touch</contact>.
getInTouch.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “Sorry, we’re having problems”.

enterTheirName.description: Label for the name input on the “Add an author” form.

enterTheirEmail.description: Label for the email input on the “Add an author” form.

invalidEmail.message: Enter an email address in the correct format, like name@example.com
invalidEmail.description: Error message that appears when the user submits the “Add an author” form with an invalid email address.

addAuthor.description: Title of the form that allows the user to add an author to the PREreview.

enterNamesAndEmailAddress.message: Enter names and email address of the other authors
enterNamesAndEmailAddress.description: Title for the form that allows the user to enter the names and email addresses of the other authors of the PREreview

enterNamesAndEmailAddressTip.message: Put each author on their own line.
enterNamesAndEmailAddressTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Enter names and email address of the other authors” heading.

namesAndEmailAddressInvalidFormat.message: Enter the author names and email addresses in the correct format
namesAndEmailAddressInvalidFormat.description: Error message that appears when the user submits the “Enter names and email address of the other authors” form with an invalid format.

namesAndEmailAddressMissing.message: Enter the author names and email addresses
namesAndEmailAddressMissing.description: Error message that appears when the user submits the “Enter names and email address of the other authors” form without entering any names or email addresses.

nameInputLabel.description: Label for the name input on the “Add an author” form.

theyWillChooseTheirName.message: They will be able to choose their published name.
theyWillChooseTheirName.description: Text shown alongside the “Name” label on the “Add an author” form.

emailAddress.description: Label for the email input on the “Add an author” form.

useOfEmail.message: We’ll only use this to contact them about this PREreview.
useOfEmail.description: Text shown alongside the “Email address” label on the “Add an author” form.

howWouldYouLikeToStart.message: How would you like to start your PREreview?
howWouldYouLikeToStart.description: Heading for the page that asks the user how they would like to start their PREreview.

selectHowToStart.message: Select how you would like to start your PREreview
selectHowToStart.description: Error message that appears when the user submits the “How would you like to start your PREreview?” page without selecting an option.

withPrompts.description: Option for the “How would you like to start your PREreview?” question to create a Structured PREreview.

withTemplate.description: Option for the “How would you like to start your PREreview?” question to use a template.

alreadyWritten.description: Option for the “How would you like to start your PREreview?” question to paste an existing review.

weWillAskQuestions.message: We’ll ask questions about the preprint to create a structured review.
weWillAskQuestions.description: Text that appears alongside the “With prompts” option for the “How would you like to start your PREreview?” question.

weWillTemplate.message: We’ll offer a basic template, but you can review it your way.
weWillTemplate.description: Text that appears alongside the “With a template” option for the “How would you like to start your PREreview?” question.

pasteYourReview.description: Heading for the page that allows the user to paste an existing review.

writeYourReview.description: Heading for the page that allows the user to write a new review.

selectTheNameError.message: Select the name that you would like to use
selectTheNameError.description: Error message that appears when the user submits the “What name would you like to use?” form without selecting an option.

youCanChooseBetweenNames.message: You can choose between the name on your ORCID record or your PREreview pseudonym.
youCanChooseBetweenNames.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “What name would you like to use?”.

whatIsAPseudonym.description: Heading for the section that explains what a PREreview pseudonym is.

pseudonymIsA.message: A <term>PREreview pseudonym</term> is an alternate name you can use instead of your real name. It is unique and combines a random color and animal. Your pseudonym is ‘{pseudonym}.’
pseudonymIsA.description: Text following the “What is a PREreview pseudonym?” heading explaining what a PREreview pseudonym is.

pseudonymAccountability.message: Using your pseudonym, you can contribute to open preprint review without fearing retribution or judgment that may occur when using your real name. However, using a pseudonym retains an element of accountability.
pseudonymAccountability.description: Text following the “What is a PREreview pseudonym?” heading explaining why you might choose to use a PREreview pseudonym.

linkToOrcidId.message: We’ll link your PREreview to your ORCID iD.
linkToOrcidId.description: Text that accompanies the public persona choice for the question “What name would you like to use?”.

linkToPseudonym.message: We’ll only link your PREreview to others that also use your pseudonym.
linkToPseudonym.description: Text that accompanies the pseudonym choice for the question “What name would you like to use?”.

pasteYourReviewError.description: Error message that appears when the user submits the “Paste your PREreview” form without entering any text.

enterYourReviewError.description: Error message that appears when the user submits the “Enter your PREreview” form without entering any text.

copyAndPasteReview.message: Copy your PREreview and paste it here. We’ll do our best to preserve how it looks.
copyAndPasteReview.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Paste your PREreview” explaining that the user can copy and paste their review into the text area.

writeReviewSupport.message: We want to support you in contributing high-quality feedback on PREreview. Check out our <link>tips and resources for reviewers</link>.
writeReviewSupport.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Write your PREreview” explaining that the user can check out tips and resources for reviewers.

goodBehaviorExamples.description: Heading for the section alongside the “Write your PREreview” heading that explains what good reviewer behavior looks like.

goodBehaviorRespectful.message: Being respectful of the authors and their work.
goodBehaviorRespectful.description: A behavior shown in the “Examples of good reviewer behavior” list for the “Write your PREreview” form.

goodBehaviorHumble.message: Being humble and aware of how you would like to receive feedback from others.
goodBehaviorHumble.description: A behavior shown in the “Examples of good reviewer behavior” list for the “Write your PREreview” form.

goodBehaviorActionable.message: Giving clear, constructive, and actionable feedback that can improve the preprint.
goodBehaviorActionable.description: A behavior shown in the “Examples of good reviewer behavior” list for the “Write your PREreview” form.

reviewSectionsExamples.description: Heading for the section alongside the “Write your PREreview” heading that provides examples of helpful review sections.

beginSummary.message: Begin with a summary of the research and how it contributes to the field of study.
beginSummary.description: A step shown in the “Examples of helpful review sections” list for the “Write your PREreview” form.

sharePositiveFeedback.message: Next, share your positive feedback, including the approach’s strengths and results.
sharePositiveFeedback.description: A step shown in the “Examples of helpful review sections” list for the “Write your PREreview” form.

shareConcerns.message: Finally, share major and minor concerns and related clear, constructive, and actionable suggestions for addressing them.
shareConcerns.description: A step shown in the “Examples of helpful review sections” list for the “Write your PREreview” form.

writeAShortSummary.message: Write a short summary of the research’s main findings and how this work has moved the field forward.
writeAShortSummary.description: The opening part of the “Write your PREreview” template.

majorIssues.description: Heading for the section of the “Write your PREreview” template that asks the user to list major issues with the preprint.

listSignificantConcerns.message: List significant concerns about the research, if there are any.
listSignificantConcerns.description: Text shown alongside the “Major issues” heading in the “Write your PREreview” template.

minorIssues.description: Heading for the section of the “Write your PREreview” template that asks the user to list minor issues with the preprint.

listFlowConcerns.message: List concerns that would improve the overall flow or clarity but are not critical to the understanding and conclusions of the research.
listFlowConcerns.description: Text shown alongside the “Minor issues” heading in the “Write your PREreview” template.

addedAuthorCount.message: {authorCount, plural, one {You have added # other author} other {You have added # other authors}}
addedAuthorCount.description: Text showing how many other authors have been added to the PREreview.

authorNumber.description: Label for the author number in the list of authors on the PREreview.

changeAuthorDetailsLink.description: Link that allows the user to change the details of an author on the PREreview.

removeAuthor.description: Link that allows the user to remove an author from the PREreview.

name.description: Label for the name of an author on the PREreview.

addAnotherAuthor.description: Link that allows the user to add another author to the PREreview.

no.description: Label for a negative answer to a question.

yes.description: Label for a positive answer to a question.

enterName.description: Error message that appears when the user submits the “Add an author” form without entering a name.

enterEmail.description: Error message that appears when the user submits the “Add an author” form without entering an email address.

changeAuthorDetailsHeading.description: Link to change an author’s details.

ableToChoseName.message: They will be able to choose their published name.
ableToChoseName.description: Text shown alongside the “Add an author” heading.

prereviewPublishedTitle.description: Heading for the page that appears after the user has published their PREreview.

yourDoi.description: Label for the DOI of the PREreview on the “PREreview published” page.

whatHappensNext.description: Heading for details of what happens next and when on the “PREreview published” page; the user has completed their task and has no further actions to take.

whereYouCanSeeYourPrereview.message: You’ll be able to see your PREreview shortly. It’ll also appear on our <communitySlack>Community Slack</communitySlack>.
whereYouCanSeeYourPrereview.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “What happens next” explaining where the user can see their PREreview.

whereYouCanSeeYourPrereviewSciety.message: You’ll be able to see your PREreview shortly. It’ll also appear on our <communitySlack>Community Slack</communitySlack> and <sciety>Sciety</sciety>.
whereYouCanSeeYourPrereviewSciety.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “What happens next” explaining where the user can see their PREreview.

opensInNewTab.description: Text that appears alongside a link where it will open in a new tab.

letUsKnowAuthorDetails.message: Please let us know the other authors’ details (names and ORCID iDs), and we’ll add them to the PREreview. Our email address is {mailtoHelp}.
letUsKnowAuthorDetails.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “What happens next” explaining that the user can let PREreview know the other authors’ details.

sentEmailsToAuthors.message: We’ve sent emails to the other authors, inviting them to appear.
sentEmailsToAuthors.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “What happens next” explaining that PREreview has sent emails to the other authors.

shareYourReview.description: Heading for the section on the “PREreview published” page that explains how the user can share their PREreview.

letAuthorsKnow.message: Please let the authors know that you published your review. They may have contact details on the preprint.
letAuthorsKnow.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “Share your review” on the “PREreview published” page.

letCommunityKnow.message: Also, why not let the wider community know:
letCommunityKnow.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “Share your review” on the “PREreview published” page.

shareOnBluesky.description: Button that allows the user to share their PREreview on Bluesky on the “PREreview published” page.

shareOnBlueskyMessage.message: I’ve just published a #PreprintReview of {preprintTitle} on {prereviewHandle}
shareOnBlueskyMessage.description: Suggested message to share on Bluesky after publishing a PREreview.

shareOnLinkedin.description: Button that allows the user to share their PREreview on LinkedIn on the “PREreview published” page.

listOnSciety.description: Button that allows the user to list their PREreview on Sciety on the “PREreview published” page.

howItWent.message: Let us know how it went
howItWent.description: Heading for the section on the “PREreview published” page that explains how the user can provide feedback about their experience on PREreview.

scheduleAnInterview.message: <link>Schedule an interview</link> with our product team to discuss your experience on PREreview. We gladly compensate interviewees in appreciation for their help!
scheduleAnInterview.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “Let us know how it went” on the “PREreview published” page.

feedbackSurvey.message: You can also share your feedback about PREreview.org with us by completing a <link>brief anonymous survey</link>.
feedbackSurvey.description: Prompt that appears alongside the heading “Let us know how it went” on the “PREreview published” page, containing a link that takes you to a form where you can share feedback about PREreview.org (what worked well on the site, what didn’t, etc.).

backToPreprint.description: Link that takes the user back to the preprint page.

publishTitle.description: Heading for the page that allows the user to publish their PREreview.

checkPrereview.description: Heading for the page that allows the user to check their PREreview before publishing.

preprintDetails.description: Heading for the section on the “Check your PREreview” page that shows the details of the preprint.

preprintTitle.description: Label for the title of the preprint in the “Preprint details” section on the “Check your PREreview” page.

preprintServer.description: Label for the preprint server in the “Preprint details” section on the “Check your PREreview” page.

authorDetails.description: Heading for the section on the “Check your PREreview” page that shows the details of the authors of the preprint.

yourDetails.description: Heading for the section on the “Check your PREreview” page that shows the details of the user.

yourPublishedName.description: Label for the published name of the user in the “Your details” section on the “Check your PREreview” page.

publishedName.description: Label for the published name of the user in the “Author details” section on the “Check your PREreview” page.

changeName.description: Link that allows the user to change their published name on the “Check your PREreview” page.

invitedAuthors.description: Label for the invited authors of the preprint in the “Author details” section on the “Check your PREreview” page.

changeInvitedAuthors.description: Link that allows the user to change the invited authors on the “Check your PREreview” page.

changeCompetingInterests.description: Link that allows the user to change their competing interests on the “Check your PREreview” page.

changePrereview.description: Link that allows the user to change their PREreview text on the “Check your PREreview” page.

yourReview.description: Heading for the section on the “Check your PREreview” page that shows the text of the PREreview.

doesIntroductionExplain.message: Does the introduction explain the objective of the research presented in the preprint?
doesIntroductionExplain.description: Question that asks the user if the introduction explains the objective of the research presented in the preprint.

changeIfIntroExplains.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the introduction explains the objective of the research presented in the preprint</visuallyHidden>
changeIfIntroExplains.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Does the introduction explain the objective of the research presented in the preprint?” on the “Check your PREreview” page.

methodsWellSuited.message: Are the methods well-suited for this research?
methodsWellSuited.description: Question that asks the user if the methods are well-suited for the research presented in the preprint.

changeMethodsWellSuited.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the methods are well-suited for this research</visuallyHidden>
changeMethodsWellSuited.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” on the “Check your PREreview” page.

conclusionsSupported.message: Are the conclusions supported by the data?
conclusionsSupported.description: Question that asks the user if the conclusions are supported by the data presented in the preprint.

changeConclusionsSupported.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the conclusions are supported by the data</visuallyHidden>
changeConclusionsSupported.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” on the “Check your PREreview” page.

dataPresentationWellSuited.message: Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?
dataPresentationWellSuited.description: Question that asks the user if the data presentations, including visualizations, are well-suited to represent the data presented in the preprint.

changeDataPresentationWellSuited.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the data presentations are well-suited to represent the data?</visuallyHidden>
changeDataPresentationWellSuited.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” on the “Check your PREreview” page.

clearDiscussion.message: How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?
clearDiscussion.description: Question that asks the user how clearly the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research presented in the preprint.

changeClearDiscussion.message: Change <visuallyHidden>how clearly the authors discuss their findings and next steps</visuallyHidden>
changeClearDiscussion.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” on the “Check your PREreview” page.

advanceKnowledge.message: Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?
advanceKnowledge.description: Question that asks the user if the preprint is likely to advance academic knowledge.

changeAdvanceKnowledge.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the preprint is likely to advance academic knowledge</visuallyHidden>
changeAdvanceKnowledge.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” on the “Check your PREreview” page.

benefitFromEditing.message: Would it benefit from language editing?
benefitFromEditing.description: Question that asks the user if the preprint would benefit from language editing.

changeBenefitFromEditing.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if it would benefit from language editing</visuallyHidden>
changeBenefitFromEditing.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Would it benefit from language editing?” on the “Check your PREreview” page.

wouldRecommend.message: Would you recommend this preprint to others?
wouldRecommend.description: Question that asks the user if they would recommend this preprint to others.

changeWouldRecommend.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if you would recommend this preprint to others</visuallyHidden>
changeWouldRecommend.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Would you recommend this preprint to others?” on the “Check your PREreview” page.

readyForAttention.message: Is it ready for attention from an editor, publisher or broader audience?
readyForAttention.description: Question that asks the user if the preprint is ready for attention from an editor, publisher or broader audience.

changeReadyForAttention.message: Change <visuallyHidden>it it is ready for attention from an editor, publisher or broader audience</visuallyHidden>
changeReadyForAttention.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Is it ready for attention from an editor, publisher or broader audience?” on the “Check your PREreview” page.

nowPublish.description: Heading for the section to publish the PREreview after checking their answers.

weWillAssignLicense.message: We will assign your PREreview a DOI (a permanent identifier) and make it publicly available under a {licenseLink} license.
weWillAssignLicense.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Now publish your PREreview” explaining what will happen when the user publishes their PREreview.

publishButton.description: Button that allows the user to publish their PREreview on the “Check your PREreview” page.

selectYesToRemove.message: Select yes if you want to remove {authorName}
selectYesToRemove.description: Error message that appears when the user submits the “Are you sure you want to remove {authorName}?” form without selecting an option.

sureYouWantToRemove.message: Are you sure you want to remove {authorName}?
sureYouWantToRemove.description: Heading for the page that asks the user if they are sure they want to remove an author from the PREreview.

yesRemove.description: Label for an option to the “Are you sure you want to remove {authorName}?” question.

writeAPrereview.description: Title for the page where a user can start the review-a-preprint flow.

authoredBy.description: Text for a preprint showing its authors.

posted.description: Label for the date a preprint was posted.

server.description: Label for the preprint server where the preprint is published.

itemId.description: Label for the unique identifier of a preprint on PhilSci-Archive.

youCanWriteAPrereview.message: You can write a PREreview of {preprintTitle}. A PREreview is a review of a preprint and can vary from a few sentences to a lengthy report, similar to a journal-organized peer-review report.
youCanWriteAPrereview.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “Write a PREreview” explaining what a PREreview is.

beforeStartHeading.description: Heading for the section that contains information about what the user needs to know before starting a PREreview.

orcidLogIn.message: We will ask you to log in with your ORCID iD. If you don’t have an iD, you can create one.
orcidLogIn.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Before you start”.

whatIsOrcidHeading.description: Heading for the explanation of what an ORCID iD is.

whatIsOrcid.message: An <link>ORCID iD</link> is a unique identifier that distinguishes you from everyone with the same or similar name.
whatIsOrcid.description: Text following the heading “What is an ORCID iD?” explaining what an ORCID iD is.

asYouHaveAlreadyStarted.message: As you’ve already started a PREreview of <cite>{preprintTitle}</cite>, we’ll take you to the next step so you can carry on.
asYouHaveAlreadyStarted.description: Message shown alongside the title “Write a PREreview when the user has already started a PREreview of the preprint.

selectYesIfYouReviewedWithSomeoneElse.message: Select yes if you reviewed the preprint with someone else
selectYesIfYouReviewedWithSomeoneElse.description: Error message that appears when the user submits the “Did you review this preprint with anyone else?” question without selecting an option.

confirmOtherAuthorsHaveReadAndApproved.message: Confirm that the other authors have read and approved the PREreview
confirmOtherAuthorsHaveReadAndApproved.description: Error message that appears when the user submits the “Did you review this preprint with anyone else?” question and selects “Yes” but does not confirm that the other authors have read and approved the PREreview.

didYouReviewWithAnyoneElse.message: Did you review this preprint with anyone else?
didYouReviewWithAnyoneElse.description: Question that asks the user if they reviewed the preprint with anyone else.

thisCanIncludePeopleWho.message: This can include people who contributed to the discussion or wrote the review.
thisCanIncludePeopleWho.description: Text that appears alongside the question “Did you review this preprint with anyone else?” explaining who can be included as other authors.

noIReviewedAlone.description: Label for the option to the question “Did you review this preprint with anyone else?” that indicates the user reviewed the preprint alone.

yesButDoNotWantToBeListed.message: Yes, but they don’t want to be listed as authors
yesButDoNotWantToBeListed.description: Label for the option to the question “Did you review this preprint with anyone else?” that indicates the other authors do not want to be listed.

yesAndWantToBeListed.message: Yes, and some or all want to be listed as authors
yesAndWantToBeListed.description: Label for the option to the question “Did you review this preprint with anyone else?” that indicates the other authors want to be listed.

theyHaveReadAndApproved.message: They have read and approved the PREreview
theyHaveReadAndApproved.description: Label for confirming that the other authors have read and approved the PREreview when selecting the “Yes, and some or all want to be listed as authors” option to the question “Did you review this preprint with anyone else?”.

isClubReview.description: Label the question where the user can add the review to a club.

asLeadCanAddToClub.message: {numberOfClubs, plural, one {As you’re a club lead, we can add the review to your club.} other {As you’re a club lead, we can add the review to one of your clubs.}}
asLeadCanAddToClub.description: Text that appears alongside the question “Is this a club review?” when the user is a lead of one or more clubs.

isClubReviewError.message: Select a club for the review
isClubReviewError.description: Error message that appears when the user submits the “Is this a club review?” question without selecting an option.

addToClub.description: Label for an option to the “Is this a club review?” question that allows the user to add the review to a club.

notClubReview.description: Label for the option to the “Is this a club review?” question that indicates the review is not to be added to a club.

club.description: Label for the club that the user has chosen to add the review to, or the text “Not in a club”.

changeClub.description: Link that allows the user to change the club that the review will be added to.

noClub.description: Text shown alongside the label “Club” when the user has chosen not to add the review to a club.

useOfAi.description: Heading for the section that contains the user’s use of AI on the PREreview.

useOfAiShort.description: Heading for the section that contains the user’s use of AI on the “Check your review” page.

discloseAiIdeas.message: We ask all reviewers to disclose the use of AI that helped them generate new ideas for their PREreview. Examples of generative AI tools are ChatGPT and Gemini.
discloseAiIdeas.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)” explaining that the user should disclose any use of AI that helped them generate new ideas for their PREreview.

aiEditing.message: You don’t need to declare AI tools used purely for language editing or accessibility needs, as long as the ideas in your PREreview are your own.
aiEditing.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)” explaining that the user does not need to declare AI tools used purely for language editing or accessibility needs.

understandHowCreated.message: We ask this to help readers understand how the review was created: whether it is human feedback, machine feedback, or a mix of both. We may remove any review with ideas entirely generated by AI.
understandHowCreated.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)” explaining why we ask the question and what might happen.

didYouUseAi.message: Did you use AI to generate ideas for this review?
didYouUseAi.description: Question that asks the user if they used AI to generate ideas for their PREreview.

didAuthorsUseAi.message: Did you, or any of the other authors, use AI to generate ideas for this review?
didAuthorsUseAi.description: Question that asks the user if they or any of the other authors used AI to generate ideas for their PREreview.

selectYesIfUsedAi.message: Select yes if you used AI to generate ideas for this review
selectYesIfUsedAi.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Did you use AI to generate ideas for this review?” form without selecting an option.

selectYesIfAuthorsUsedAi.message: Select yes if you, or any of the other authors, used AI to generate ideas for this review
selectYesIfAuthorsUsedAi.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Did you, or any of the other authors, use AI to generate ideas for this review?” form without selecting an option.

aiIdeasStatement.message: The author declares that they used generative AI to come up with new ideas for their review.
aiIdeasStatement.description: Statement added to the PREreview when the author answers Yes to the question “Did you use AI to generate ideas for this review?”.

aiIdeasAuthorsStatement.message: The authors declare that they used generative AI to come up with new ideas for their review.
aiIdeasAuthorsStatement.description: Statement added to the PREreview when the authors answer Yes to the question “Did you, or any of the other authors, use AI to generate ideas for this review?”.

noAiIdeasStatement.message: The author declares that they did not use generative AI to come up with new ideas for their review.
noAiIdeasStatement.description: Statement added to the PREreview when the author answers “No” to the question “Did you use AI to generate ideas for this review?”.

noAiIdeasAuthorsStatement.message: The authors declare that they did not use generative AI to come up with new ideas for their review.
noAiIdeasAuthorsStatement.description: Statement added to the PREreview when the authors answer “No” to the question “Did you, or any of the other authors, use AI to generate ideas for this review?”.

changeUseOfAi.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Did you use AI to generate ideas for this review?” on the “Check your PREreview” page.

whatNameToUse.message: What name would you like to use?
whatNameToUse.description: Heading for the page that asks the user what name they would like to use for their PREreview.

competingInterests.description: Heading for the section that contains the user’s competing interests on the “Check your comment” page.

competingInterestsNo.message: The author declares that they have no competing interests.
competingInterestsNo.description: Statement added to a review when there is one author and they declared that they don't have any competing interests.

competingInterestsAuthorsNo.message: The authors declare that they have no competing interests.
competingInterestsAuthorsNo.description: Statement added to a review when there are multiple authors and they declared that they don't have any competing interests.

selectYesIfCompetingInterest.message: Select yes if you have any competing interests
selectYesIfCompetingInterest.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Do you have any competing interests?” form without selecting an option.

selectYesIfAuthorsCompetingInterest.message: Select yes if you, or any of the other authors, have any competing interests
selectYesIfAuthorsCompetingInterest.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Do you, or any of the other authors, have any competing interests?” form without selecting an option.

competingInterestDetails.message: Enter details of your competing interests
competingInterestDetails.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Do you have any competing interests?” form with a “Yes” answer but without entering details.

authorsCompetingInterestDetails.message: Enter details of the competing interests
authorsCompetingInterestDetails.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Do you, or any of the other authors, have any competing interests?” form with a “Yes” answer but without entering details.

doYouHaveCompetingInterests.message: Do you have any competing interests?
doYouHaveCompetingInterests.description: Title for the page where a user can declare any competing interests they may have.

doAuthorsHaveCompetingInterests.message: Do you, or any of the other authors, have any competing interests?
doAuthorsHaveCompetingInterests.description: Title for the page where a user can declare any competing interests they, or any of the other authors, may have.

discloseCompetingInterests.message: We ask all reviewers to disclose any competing interests that could influence their review of the preprint.
discloseCompetingInterests.description: Text that appears alongside the heading “Do you have any competing interests?” explaining that the user should disclose any competing interests that could influence their review of the preprint.

whatIsCompetingInterest.message: A competing interest is anything that could interfere with the objectivity of a PREreview.
whatIsCompetingInterest.description: Information provided about the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

competingInterestsMatter.message: Competing interests matter because they can introduce perceived or actual bias in the evaluation of the preprint. Declaring them is crucial for ensuring transparency and maintaining the integrity of the review process.
competingInterestsMatter.description: Information provided about the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

examples.description: Heading for the list of examples.

conflictPersonalRelationship.message: You have a personal relationship with the author.
conflictPersonalRelationship.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

conflictRivalOfAuthor.message: You are a rival or competitor of the author.
conflictRivalOfAuthor.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

conflictRecentlyWorkedTogether.message: You have recently worked with the author.
conflictRecentlyWorkedTogether.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

conflictCollaborateWithAuthor.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

conflictPublishedTogether.message: You have published with the author in the last five years.
conflictPublishedTogether.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

conflictHoldGrantTogether.message: You hold a grant with the author.
conflictHoldGrantTogether.description: A competing interest shown in the list of examples for the question “Do you have any competing interests?”.

whatAreThey.description: Label for the input for providing details having selected the “Yes” option for the “Do you have any competing interests?” question.

confirmCodeOfConduct.message: Confirm that you are following the Code of Conduct
confirmCodeOfConduct.description: Error message shown when a user tries to submit the “Code of Conduct” form without ticking the “I’m following the Code of Conduct” checkbox.

codeOfConduct.description: Title for the page where a user can confirm that they are following the Code of Conduct.

expectYouToAbideByCodeOfConduct.message: As a member of our community, we expect you to abide by the <link>PREreview Code of Conduct</link>.
expectYouToAbideByCodeOfConduct.description: Information provided for the “Code of Conduct” form.

examplesOfExpectedBehavior.description: Heading for the list of examples of expected behaviors for the “Code of Conduct” form.

expectedBehaviorLanguage.description: An expected behavior shown in the “Examples of expected behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

expectedBehaviorFeedback.message: Providing feedback that is constructive, i.e. useful, to the receiver.
expectedBehaviorFeedback.description: An expected behavior shown in the “Examples of expected behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

expectedBehaviorRespect.message: Being respectful of differing viewpoints and experiences.
expectedBehaviorRespect.description: An expected behavior shown in the “Examples of expected behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

expectedBehaviorGracefulAcceptance.description: An expected behavior shown in the “Examples of expected behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

expectedBehaviorBestOfCommunity.message: Focusing on what is best for the community.
expectedBehaviorBestOfCommunity.description: An expected behavior shown in the “Examples of expected behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

expectedBehaviorEmpathy.message: Showing empathy towards other community members.
expectedBehaviorEmpathy.description: An expected behavior shown in the “Examples of expected behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

examplesOfUnacceptableBehavior.description: Heading for the list of examples of unacceptable behaviors for the “Code of Conduct” form.

unacceptableBehaviorTrollingEtc.message: Trolling, insulting or derogatory comments, and personal or political attacks.
unacceptableBehaviorTrollingEtc.description: An unacceptable behavior shown in the “Examples of unacceptable behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

unacceptableBehaviorUnconstructive.message: Providing unconstructive or disruptive feedback on PREreview.
unacceptableBehaviorUnconstructive.description: An unacceptable behavior shown in the “Examples of unacceptable behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

unacceptableBehaviorHarassment.description: An unacceptable behavior shown in the “Examples of unacceptable behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

unacceptableBehaviorPublishingConfidenti.message: Publishing others’ confidential information, such as a physical or electronic address, without explicit permission.
unacceptableBehaviorPublishingConfidenti.description: An unacceptable behavior shown in the “Examples of unacceptable behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

unacceptableBehaviorSexualizedLanguage.message: Use of sexualized language or imagery and unwelcome sexual attention or advances.
unacceptableBehaviorSexualizedLanguage.description: An unacceptable behavior shown in the “Examples of unacceptable behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

unacceptableBehaviorReviewingOwnPreprint.description: An unacceptable behavior shown in the “Examples of unacceptable behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

unacceptableBehaviorInappropriate.message: Other conduct which could reasonably be considered inappropriate in a professional setting.
unacceptableBehaviorInappropriate.description: An unacceptable behavior shown in the “Examples of unacceptable behaviors” list for the “Code of Conduct” form.

iAmFollowingCodeOfConduct.message: I’m following the Code of Conduct
iAmFollowingCodeOfConduct.description: Label for the checkbox in the “Code of Conduct” form.

contactDetails.description: Title for the page where a user can provide their contact details.

enterEmailAddressError.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Contact details” form without entering an email address.

enterEmailAddressFormatError.message: Enter an email address in the correct format, like name@example.com
enterEmailAddressFormatError.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Contact details” form with an invalid email address.

confirmEmailAddress.message: We’re ready to publish your PREreview, but we need to confirm your email address first.
confirmEmailAddress.description: Text that appears alongside the “Contact details” title explaining that the user needs to confirm their email address before they can publish their PREreview.

onlyUseContact.message: We’ll only use this to contact you about your account and PREreviews.
onlyUseContact.description: Text that appears alongside the “Contact details” title explaining how the user’s email address will be used.

whatIsYourEmail.description: Label for the input field for the user’s email address on the “Contact details” form.

verifyEmailAddress.description: Title for the page after the “Contact details” form.

howToVerifyEmailAddress.message: Please open the email we sent to {emailAddress} and follow the link. You’ll then be able to publish your PREreview.
howToVerifyEmailAddress.description: Text that appears alongside the title “Verify your email address” and explains what the user needs to do next.

onceEmailAddressVerified.message: Once your address is verified, you can close this page.
onceEmailAddressVerified.description: Text that appears alongside the title “Verify your email address” and informs the user that they can close the page once their email address is verified.

resendEmailButton.description: Label for the button that sends the verification email again on the “Verify your email address” page.

selectIntroductionExplains.message: Select if the introduction explains the objective of the research presented in the preprint
selectIntroductionExplains.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Does the introduction explain the objective of the research presented in the preprint?” form without selecting an option.

clearlyExplainsTip.description: Label for an option to the “Does the introduction explain the objective of the research presented in the preprint?” question.

howIntroductionExplains.message: How does the introduction explain the objective? (optional)
howIntroductionExplains.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “It clearly explains the objective” answer to the “Does the introduction explain the objective of the research presented in the preprint?” question.

partly.description: Label for an option to a question.

partlyTip.message: It mentions, but doesn’t fully explain, the objective.
partlyTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Partly” option to the “Does the introduction explain the objective of the research presented in the preprint?” question.

partlyHow.message: How does the introduction only partly explain the objective? (optional)
partlyHow.description: Label for the textbox to explain the ““Partly” answer to the “Does the introduction explain the objective of the research presented in the preprint?” question.

doesNotExplain.message: It doesn’t mention or explain the objective.
doesNotExplain.description: Label for an option to the “Does the introduction explain the objective of the research presented in the preprint?” question.

doesNotExplainHow.message: How does the introduction not explain the objective? (optional)
doesNotExplainHow.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “It doesn’t mention or explain the objective” answer to the “Does the introduction explain the objective of the research presented in the preprint?” question.

selectMethodsWellSuited.message: Select if the methods are well-suited for this research
selectMethodsWellSuited.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” form without selecting an option.

selectClearDiscussion.message: Select how clearly the authors discuss their findings and next steps
selectClearDiscussion.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” form without selecting an option.

methodsHighlyAppropriate.description: Label for an option to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsHighlyAppropriateTip.message: They follow best practices throughout the research. They are rigorously executed and provide a robust foundation for drawing valid conclusions.
methodsHighlyAppropriateTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Highly appropriate” option to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsHighlyAppropriateHow.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Highly appropriate” answer to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsSomewhatAppropriate.description: Label for an option to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsSomewhatAppropriateTip.message: They follow best practices through most, but not all, of the research. They are well executed and provide a good foundation for drawing valid conclusions.
methodsSomewhatAppropriateTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Somewhat appropriate” option to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsSomewhatAppropriateWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Somewhat appropriate” answer to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsNeitherAppropriateNorInappropriat.description: Label for an option to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsNeitherAppropriateNorInappropriat.message: They do not always follow best practices but give a reasonable basis for answering the research question.
methodsNeitherAppropriateNorInappropriat.description: Text shown alongside the “Neither appropriate nor inappropriate” option to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsNeitherAppropriateNorInappropriat.message: Why are they neither appropriate nor inappropriate? (optional)
methodsNeitherAppropriateNorInappropriat.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Neither appropriate nor inappropriate” answer to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsSomewhatInappropriate.description: Label for an option to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsSomewhatInappropriateTip.message: They have certain flaws or deviations from best practices that limit the value of the information and insights they share.
methodsSomewhatInappropriateTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Somewhat inappropriate” option to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsSomewhatInappropriateWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Somewhat inappropriate” answer to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsHighlyInappropriate.description: Label for an option to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsHighlyInappropriateTip.message: They are fundamentally flawed, invalid, or inconsistent with best practices.
methodsHighlyInappropriateTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Highly inappropriate” option to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

methodsHighlyInappropriateWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Highly inappropriate” answer to the “Are the methods well-suited for this research?” question.

selectConclusionsSupported.message: Select if the conclusions are supported by the data
selectConclusionsSupported.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” form without selecting an option.

conclusionsHighlySupported.description: Label for an option to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsHighlySupportedTip.message: The conclusions are consistently thorough and provide a realistic interpretation of the data without overreaching or drawing conclusions not reflected in the results.
conclusionsHighlySupportedTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Highly supported” option to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsHighlySupportedWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Highly supported” answer to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsSomewhatSupported.description: Label for an option to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsSomewhatSupportedTip.message: The conclusions are mostly, but not always, thorough. They provide a reasonable interpretation of the data without overreaching or adding interpretations not reflected in the results.
conclusionsSomewhatSupportedTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Somewhat supported” option to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsSomewhatSupportedWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Somewhat supported” answer to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsNeitherSupportedNorUnsupporte.description: Label for an option to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsNeitherSupportedNorUnsupporte.message: No conclusions have been drawn about the data, or those presented are reasonable but not well explained or justified by the authors.
conclusionsNeitherSupportedNorUnsupporte.description: Text shown alongside the “Neither supported nor unsupported” option to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsNeitherSupportedNorUnsupporte.message: Why are they neither supported nor unsupported? (optional)
conclusionsNeitherSupportedNorUnsupporte.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Neither supported nor unsupported” answer to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsSomewhatUnsupported.description: Label for an option to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsSomewhatUnsupportedTip.message: The conclusions do not entirely address the data, or the conclusions overgeneralize and are not well-supported by the data.
conclusionsSomewhatUnsupportedTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Somewhat unsupported” option to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsSomewhatUnsupportedWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Somewhat unsupported” answer to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsHighlyUnsupported.description: Label for an option to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsHighlyUnsupportedTip.message: The conclusions do not follow from the data and are unsupported or contradicted by it.
conclusionsHighlyUnsupportedTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Highly unsupported” option to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

conclusionsHighlyUnsupportedWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Highly unsupported” answer to the “Are the conclusions supported by the data?” question.

veryClearly.description: Label for an option to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

veryClearlyTip.message: They demonstrate clarity, depth, and insight in their discussion, explanation, and interpretation of their findings and potential next steps.
veryClearlyTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Very clearly” option to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

veryClearlyHow.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Very clearly” answer to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

somewhatClearly.description: Label for an option to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

somewhatClearlyTip.message: They provide clear and insightful discussion, explanation, and interpretation of their findings and potential next steps.
somewhatClearlyTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Somewhat clearly” option to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

somewhatClearlyHow.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Somewhat clearly” answer to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

neitherClearlyNorUnclearly.description: Label for an option to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

neitherClearlyNorUnclearlyTip.message: They mention their findings or potential next steps, but do not address or explain them.
neitherClearlyNorUnclearlyTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Neither clearly nor unclearly” option to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

neitherClearlyNorUnclearlyHow.message: How is it neither clear nor unclear? (optional)
neitherClearlyNorUnclearlyHow.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Neither clearly nor unclearly” answer to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

somewhatUnclearly.description: Label for an option to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

somewhatUnclearlyTip.message: They provide confusing or contradictory discussion, explanation, or interpretation of their findings and potential next steps.
somewhatUnclearlyTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Somewhat unclearly” option to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

somewhatUnclearlyHow.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Somewhat unclearly” answer to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

veryUnclearly.description: Label for an option to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

veryUnclearlyTip.message: They provide incorrect or unfounded discussion, explanation, or interpretation of their findings and potential next steps.
veryUnclearlyTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Very unclearly” option to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

veryUnclearlyHow.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Very unclearly” answer to the “How clearly do the authors discuss, explain, and interpret their findings and potential next steps for the research?” question.

selectAdvanceKnowledge.message: Select if the preprint is likely to advance academic knowledge
selectAdvanceKnowledge.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” form without selecting an option.

advanceKnowledgeHighlyLikely.description: Label for an option to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeHighlyLikelyTip.message: The preprint offers significant contributions that substantially advance or confirm understanding of the subject matter.
advanceKnowledgeHighlyLikelyTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Highly likely” option to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeHighlyLikelyWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Highly likely” answer to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeSomewhatLikely.description: Label for an option to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeSomewhatLikelyTip.message: The preprint contributes several noteworthy advancements or confirmations.
advanceKnowledgeSomewhatLikelyTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Somewhat likely” option to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeSomewhatLikelyWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Somewhat likely” answer to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeModeratelyLikely.description: Label for an option to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeModeratelyLikelyTip.message: The preprint contributes only a few advancements or confirmations to the existing body of knowledge.
advanceKnowledgeModeratelyLikelyTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Moderately likely” option to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeModeratelyLikelyWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Moderately likely” answer to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeNotLikely.description: Label for an option to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeNotLikelyTip.message: The preprint offers no significant advancements or confirmations, though its research and conclusions may be sound.
advanceKnowledgeNotLikelyTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Not likely” option to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeNotLikelyWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Not likely” answer to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeNotAtAllLikely.description: Label for an option to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeNotAtAllLikelyTip.message: The preprint offers no significant advancements or confirmations because of its flaws.
advanceKnowledgeNotAtAllLikelyTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Not at all likely” option to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

advanceKnowledgeNotAtAllLikelyWhy.message: Why is it not at all likely? (optional)
advanceKnowledgeNotAtAllLikelyWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Not at all likely” answer to the “Is the preprint likely to advance academic knowledge?” question.

selectBenefitFromEditing.message: Select yes if it would benefit from language editing
selectBenefitFromEditing.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Would it benefit from language editing?” form without selecting an option.

benefitFromEditingNoTip.message: There may be minor language issues, but they do not impact clarity or understanding.
benefitFromEditingNoTip.description: Text shown alongside the “No” option to the “Would it benefit from language editing?” question.

benefitFromEditingNoWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “No” answer to the “Would it benefit from language editing?” question.

benefitFromEditingYesTip.message: Grammatical errors, confusing phrasing, or unclear expressions may hinder comprehension.
benefitFromEditingYesTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Yes” option to the “Would it benefit from language editing?” question.

benefitFromEditingYesWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Yes” answer to the “Would it benefit from language editing?” question.

selectWouldRecommend.message: Select yes if you would recommend this preprint to others
selectWouldRecommend.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Would you recommend this preprint to others?” form without selecting an option.

wouldRecommendYes.description: Label for an option to the “Would you recommend this preprint to others?” question.

wouldRecommendYesHow.message: How is it of high quality? (optional)
wouldRecommendYesHow.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Yes, it’s of high quality” answer to the “Would you recommend this preprint to others?” question.

wouldRecommendYesImproved.message: Yes, but it needs to be improved
wouldRecommendYesImproved.description: Label for an option to the “Would you recommend this preprint to others?” question.

wouldRecommendYesImprovedWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Yes, but it needs to be improved” answer to the “Would you recommend this preprint to others?” question.

wouldRecommendNo.message: No, it’s of low quality or is majorly flawed
wouldRecommendNo.description: Label for an option to the “Would you recommend this preprint to others?” question.

wouldRecommendNoWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “No, it’s of low quality or is majorly flawed” answer to the “Would you recommend this preprint to others?” question.

selectReadyForAttention.message: Select yes if it is ready for attention from an editor, publisher or broader audience
selectReadyForAttention.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Is it ready for attention from an editor, publisher or broader audience?” form without selecting an option.

readyForAttentionYes.description: Label for an option to the “Is it ready for attention from an editor, publisher or broader audience?” question.

readyForAttentionYesWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Yes, as it is” answer to the “Is it ready for attention from an editor, publisher or broader audience?” question.

readyForAttentionMinorChanges.description: Label for an option to the “Is it ready for attention from an editor, publisher or broader audience?” question.

readyForAttentionMinorChangesWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Yes, after minor changes” answer to the “Is it ready for attention from an editor, publisher or broader audience?” question.

readyForAttentionNo.message: No, it needs a major revision
readyForAttentionNo.description: Label for an option to the “Is it ready for attention from an editor, publisher or broader audience?” question.

readyForAttentionNoWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “No, it needs a major revision” answer to the “Is it ready for attention from an editor, publisher or broader audience?” question.

areTheDataPresentationsWellSuited.message: Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?
areTheDataPresentationsWellSuited.description: Question that asks the reviewer to evaluate whether the data presentations, including visualizations, are well-suited to represent the data.

selectIfDataPresentationsWellSuited.message: Select if the data presentations are well-suited to represent the data?
selectIfDataPresentationsWellSuited.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” form without selecting an option.

highlyAppropriateAndClear.description: Label for an option to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

highlyAppropriateAndClearTip.message: They thoroughly follow accessibility best practices and effectively communicate the results and key patterns in the data, making it very easy to comprehend or interpret the data.
highlyAppropriateAndClearTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Highly appropriate and clear” option to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

highlyAppropriateAndClearWhy.message: Why are they highly appropriate and clear? (optional)
highlyAppropriateAndClearWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Highly appropriate and clear” answer to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

somewhatAppropriate.description: Label for an option to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

somewhatAppropriateTip.message: They follow accessibility best practices and well communicate the results and main patterns in the data, making it easy to comprehend or interpret the data effectively.
somewhatAppropriateTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Somewhat appropriate and clear” option to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

somewhatAppropriateWhy.message: Why are they somewhat appropriate and clear? (optional)
somewhatAppropriateWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Somewhat appropriate and clear” answer to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

neitherAppropriateOrClear.message: Neither appropriate and clear nor inappropriate and unclear
neitherAppropriateOrClear.description: Label for an option to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

neitherAppropriateOrClearTip.message: They follow some elements of accessibility best practices and communicate the results and patterns. However, the presentations chosen are not the best or clearest ones to use for this kind of data.
neitherAppropriateOrClearTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Neither appropriate and clear nor inappropriate and unclear” option to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

neitherAppropriateOrClearWhy.message: Why are they neither appropriate and clear nor inappropriate and unclear? (optional)
neitherAppropriateOrClearWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Neither appropriate and clear nor inappropriate and unclear” answer to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

somewhatInappropriate.description: Label for an option to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

somewhatInappropriateTip.message: They don’t follow accessibility best practices, and contain minor inaccuracies, ambiguities, or omissions, making it slightly challenging to comprehend or interpret the data effectively.
somewhatInappropriateTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Somewhat inappropriate or unclear” option to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

somewhatInappropriateWhy.message: Why are they somewhat inappropriate or unclear? (optional)
somewhatInappropriateWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Somewhat inappropriate or unclear” answer to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

highlyInappropriate.description: Label for an option to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

highlyInappropriateTip.message: They present major accessibility barriers, and lack proper labeling, appropriate scales, or relevant contextual information, making it very challenging to comprehend or interpret the data effectively.
highlyInappropriateTip.description: Text shown alongside the “Highly inappropriate or unclear” option to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

highlyInappropriateWhy.message: Why are they highly inappropriate or unclear? (optional)
highlyInappropriateWhy.description: Label for the textbox to explain the “Highly inappropriate or unclear” answer to the “Are the data presentations, including visualizations, well-suited to represent the data?” question.

iDoNotKnow.description: Label for an option to a question.
